plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and comply with the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure and adhere to conservative principles.
Reduce SMH to 8% due to its downtrend and to lower sector concentration.
Increase QQQI to 25% for greater income and stability.
Buy XLV at 5% to diversify into a high-quality, less volatile sector.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall portfolio risk.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for a modest ~1.5%/month return, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but will likely have lower growth potential. Expected drawdowns are minimized.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL as it is below its stop and a leveraged ETF.
Reduce SPAL to 3% to keep leveraged ETF exposure small.
Trim SMH to 10% due to its downtrend, managing sector risk.
Buy LRCX at 8% and ASML at 5% to add high-scoring semiconductor exposure.
This plan balances growth with risk management, maintaining a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month growth, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration, while selectively adding high-quality tech. Expect moderate drawdowns, but improved diversification.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with the single-name concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL as it is below its stop, adhering to strict discipline.
Increase SPAL to 8% to lean into leveraged exposure in this mode.
Reduce SMH to 10% to manage the downtrending position.
Buy LRCX at 12% and ASML at 10% to concentrate in high-scoring semiconductor setups.
This plan aggressively pursues growth by leveraging strong scanner signals.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month growth by leaning into leverage and high-scoring setups. It carries significantly higher risk, including greater potential drawdowns and the impact of leveraged ETF decay/rebalancing. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and manage risk.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell SMH to exit the downtrending, higher-beta semiconductor sector.
Reduce CRWD to 5% to lower exposure to high-beta single names.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize monthly distributions and income focus.
This plan prioritizes steady income and low drawdown over growth.
income.read: This income plan aims for ~1%/month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. It significantly de-risks the portfolio by cutting leverage and high-beta names. While focusing on income, NAV can still fall in selloffs, and distributions are not guaranteed.
